seoTitle: Jolly Nose vs Jones Bridge — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Jolly Nose and Jones Bridge. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Jolly Nose vs Jones Bridge: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jolly Nose and Jones Bridge.
heading: Jolly Nose vs Jones Bridge — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jolly Nose, New South Wales and Jones Bridge, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jolly Nose has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Jones Bridge (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jolly Nose or Jones Bridge safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jolly Nose has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Jones Bridge scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jolly Nose scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Jones Bridge scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jolly Nose or Jones Bridge?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jolly Nose has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
